hero.title: Sustainable Development through Local Value Creation in Africa
hero.subtitle: We promote climate-resilient livelihoods, food security and nature conservation through integrated, community-led projects

about.title: About Mimea Impact
about.intro: Mimea Impact is a Swiss non-profit association founded in 2020, dedicated to sustainable development in East Africa. We believe in an integrated approach that combines community development, food security, nature conservation, and climate resilience.
about.introText: Our work is guided by rigorous scientific principles, strong local ownership, and complete transparency. We collaborate closely with local communities, academic institutions, and partner organizations to create sustainable and measurable impact.
about.missionText: We work exclusively in Africa and pursue no commercial interests
about.boardIntro: Our board brings diverse expertise in governance, law, and international project management.
about.teamIntro: Our field team combines academic expertise with deep local knowledge for effective and culturally adapted implementation.

teamMember1.name: Fabrice Volken, MSc ETH
teamMember1.bio: Fabrice holds a Master's degree in Applied Natural Sciences from ETH Zurich. As a passionate social entrepreneur, he has developed unique expertise in integrated rural development in East Africa. With several years of field experience in Kenya, he coordinates all Mimea Impact programmes and maintains strategic relationships with our local and international partners.

teamMember2.name: Esther Mrabu
teamMember2.bio: Esther brings over 8 years of experience in community mobilization and agricultural project coordination in Kilifi County. A native of the region, she has a deep understanding of local dynamics, languages, and traditions. Her extensive network and ability to build trust with communities are essential to the success of our interventions.

about.legalText: Association under Art. 60 ff. Swiss Civil Code | Headquarters: Adliswil, Zurich | Non-profit recognized

lumoDetail.title: Lumo+ | Conservation Tourism & Community Development
lumoDetail.challengeText: Communities near conservation areas face human-wildlife conflicts and lack of economic opportunities.

moringaDetail.title: Moringa Whole Value Chain | Climate, Nutrition, Water & Education
moringaDetail.challengeText: Climate change, food insecurity and contaminated water threaten rural communities.
moringaDetail.componentA: Climate-resilient agriculture (Moringa Dual Cropping + Carbon Credits)
moringaDetail.partner1: Kilifi Moringa Estate (World Bank Strategic Partner, DEG-financed)
